benchmark: solana-brain — with vs without, blind-judged A/B
method: Same base model in both conditions; the only variable is whether solana-brain is loaded. Control = no skill. Treatment = the skill (reads SKILL.md incl. the answer contract + deepened clusters; Set B also given a seeded .solana-brain memory). Uncapped answers. Blind judge scored identity-stripped A/B (A=control, B=treatment). Run via Workflow (control + treatment in parallel, then judge). Reported figures are averaged over 2 runs to smooth single-judge variance.

quality.verdict: Treatment wins every dimension; led by actionability (+1.0) and completeness (+0.6).

memory_continuity.verdict: Categorical win — the base model has no project memory; the skill recalls and honors the established Vaultis decisions (Anchor / OtterSec audit-before-mainnet / Squads 3-of-5 / Helius / Cayman+DE) and refuses to contradict a settled one.

improve_loop: v2 (capped one-shot) = parity, slightly behind. Added an 'answer contract' (route silently, answer substance-first) -> flipped to a quality win. Then a 76-finding audit deepened the clusters (canonical tx-landing path; SECURE invariant checklist; 2026 stack + Codama; named SWQoS send products + solana-verify; Howey/MiCA/entity framework) -> wins every quality dimension; memory stays 3.1x.
honest_caveats: Single LLM judge; small N — directional, not a paper. Run-to-run judge variance is real: across 2 runs treatment scored 9.0-9.25 (quality) / 9.75 (memory); control 8.35-9.05 / 2.5-3.75. The skill wins both axes in every run.
Same elite base model both sides, so quality gains are real-but-bounded (you cannot out-IQ an expert on a one-shot answer); the skill's larger, categorical edge is memory continuity.
Set B is a capability comparison: a plain LLM has no per-project memory by design.
A = control, B = treatment (fixed slots); identity hidden from the judge. No scores were hand-edited.
